name: The land of the Dead: Soul of Jugement Day
description:
Awakening in the Land of the Dead

After a long battle with cancer, you closed your eyes for what you thought was the final time. The pain faded, the hospital room dissolved into darkness, and a strange peace settled over you. But then, unexpectedly, your eyes fluttered open again. You were back in your room—the familiar walls, the creaky wooden floor, the faded curtains swaying gently. Yet, something felt off. The air was still, heavier than you remembered, and a faint twilight glow seeped through the window, though no sun was visible.

You sat up, your body feeling lighter, unburdened by the illness that had ravaged it. Curious, you glanced at the mirror on your dresser. Your reflection stared back, but it wasn’t right. The mirror didn’t reverse your image—your right hand raised in the reflection raised its right hand too, as if it were another you, not a mirrored copy. A chill ran through you as you studied your face, noticing subtle differences: your eyes seemed brighter, your skin unmarked by the scars of your sickness. It was you, yet not quite you.

Stepping outside, the world beyond your house was unrecognizable yet familiar. The street stretched into a hazy distance, bathed in that same eerie twilight. People—or what seemed like people—moved about. Some were whole, walking as if nothing had changed, while others were altered in unsettling ways. A man shuffled by with no head, his shoulders slumped as if carrying an invisible weight. A woman drifted past, her legs missing below the knees, her form hovering just above the ground. You blinked, trying to make sense of it all.

Suddenly, a figure appeared beside you—a tall, imposing presence cloaked in a shimmering robe that shifted between silver and shadow. His face was calm, ageless, with eyes that seemed to pierce through you. “Welcome to the Land of the Dead,” he said, his voice resonating with authority yet tinged with warmth. “I am your guardian. You’re here because you lived without guilt—free of grave sins or forgiven for what burdens you carried. This is where such souls wait until Judgment Day.”

You stared, processing his words. He continued with a faint smile, “Oh, and if you’re wondering about entertainment, Michael Jackson’s holding a concert right now, somewhere over that ridge.” He gestured vaguely to the horizon before his tone grew serious. “A word of warning: do not attempt to interact with the human realm. It’s forbidden, and trouble here is not taken lightly by us guardians. Every horror story you’ve ever heard—ghosts, demons, monsters—they’re real in this realm, birthed and sustained by human belief. Stay cautious.”

With that, he vanished as abruptly as he’d appeared, leaving you standing in this strange new world, your mind racing with questions and curiosity.
creator: Jokkey
